title: PeerDeliver: body_type, sent_at, reply_to, thread_root_id unbounded
description: PeerDeliver has body protected by deserialize_bounded_body (64 KiB) and message_id/chat_id by deserialize_bounded_id (128 B), but body_type, sent_at, reply_to, and thread_root_id are bare String fields with no serde length guard. A hostile MLS peer can force heap allocation of arbitrarily large strings in these fields. sent_at is stored in the daemon DB. Trigger: craft a PeerDeliver with body_type or sent_at set to a multi-MiB string. Bad outcome: unbounded heap allocation before any application check runs.
